hypothesis: Enhancing steric and electrostatic complementarity with SSTR2-unique ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions by mutating positions 5 and 11 will increase selectivity margin by differentiating SSTR2 binding from off-targets.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is insufficient to achieve consistent SSTR2 selectivity. A more targeted approach, focusing on disrupting conserved interactions in off-targets at non-pharmacophore positions, is necessary to push Δmargin positive across more candidates.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we must disrupt conserved pocket interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing on these positions allows us to design mutations that reduce off-target binding while preserving the pharmacophore core.

These mutations are designed to break conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 while avoiding disruption of the pharmacophore (positions 7-10) and Cys3/Cys14. For example, introducing charged residues at position 6 may disrupt electrostatic interactions in off-targets.
